terms.definition: A naive Bayes classifier for discrete count features where each dimension is a word (or token) count and class-conditional likelihoods follow a multinomial model over the vocabulary.
A naive Bayes classifier for binary features where each dimension indicates whether a vocabulary word is present (1) or absent (0) in a document.
A document representation where each vocabulary dimension stores how many times that token appears; the standard input to Multinomial NB.
A binary bag-of-words vector with 1 if a vocabulary word appears at least once in the document and 0 otherwise; the standard input to Bernoulli NB.
Non-negative integer features (often term frequencies or raw counts) used as Multinomial NB inputs; contrast with binary presence features.
Adding pseudo-counts (e.g., α) to word-class counts before normalizing θ_{y,i}, preventing zero probabilities for tokens unseen in training for a given class.
